senses.definition: A highway robber or bandit, especially in Spanish-speaking countries.
examples: The bandolero hid in the mountains after robbing the stagecoach.
Legends tell of the brave bandolero who stole from the rich.
Tourists love stories about the famous bandolero of the Sierra Nevada.

senses.definition: Plural of bandolero; highway robbers or bandits.
examples: The bandoleros ambushed the travelers at dawn.
Soldiers hunted the bandoleros through the rugged hills.
Old tales speak of bandoleros sharing their loot with the poor.

senses.definition: A broad belt worn over the shoulder to carry bullets or cartridges.
examples: The soldier slung his bandolier across his chest.
Cowboys in Western movies always wear a bandolier.
The bandolier held extra ammunition for the rifle.

senses.definition: Wearing a bandolier.
examples: The bandoliered warrior charged into battle.
Bandoliered rebels guarded the mountain pass.
He looked like a bandoliered bandit from an old film.

senses.definition: Plural of bandolier; belts for carrying ammunition.
examples: Soldiers filled their bandoliers with cartridges.
The museum displays antique bandoliers from World War I.
Bandoliers hung from hooks in the armory.

senses.definition: A sticky hair product used to make hair lie flat.
examples: He applied bandoline to slick back his hair.
Bandoline was popular in Victorian hairstyles.
The barber used bandoline for a smooth pompadour.

senses.definition: Treated or styled with bandoline.
examples: His hair was bandolined to perfection.
The bandolined locks gleamed under the light.
She disliked the bandolined look of old portraits.

senses.definition: Plural of bandoline; sticky hair products.
examples: Old bandolines still sit on antique dressers.
Victorians used bandolines for formal hairstyles.

senses.definition: The act of applying bandoline to hair.
examples: Bandolining was part of his morning routine.
The bandolining gave his hair a glossy finish.

senses.definition: Freedom or liberty (archaic).
examples: He lived at his own bandon.
They acted without bandon from authority.

senses.definition: A type of accordion used in tango music, popular in Argentina.
examples: The bandoneon player led the tango orchestra.
Tango without a bandoneon feels incomplete.
She learned to play the bandoneon in Buenos Aires.

senses.definition: Plural of bandoneon; tango accordions.
examples: Bandoneons filled the hall with tango sounds.
The shop sells new and used bandoneons.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of bandoneon, a tango accordion.
examples: The bandonion wailed during the milonga.
He mastered the bandonion after years of practice.

senses.definition: Plural of bandonion.
examples: Bandonions are essential in tango bands.
Collectors seek rare bandonions.

senses.definition: Slang for rifle or gun (Indian English).
examples: He aimed the bandook at the target.
The guard carried a bandook on patrol.

senses.definition: Plural of bandook; rifles or guns.
examples: Soldiers checked their bandooks before the march.
The rack held several bandooks.

senses.definition: Variant of bandore, a musical instrument.
examples: The bandor accompanied folk songs.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of bandore, a stringed instrument.
examples: The bandora was tuned for lute music.
Renaissance players favored the bandora.

senses.definition: Plural of bandora.
examples: Bandoras decorated the music room.

senses.definition: An old stringed musical instrument like a lute.
examples: The bandore was popular in the Renaissance.
He plucked the strings of the bandore softly.

senses.definition: Plural of bandore.
examples: Bandores were common in Elizabethan England.
The collection includes several bandores.

senses.definition: Official proclamations or edicts, especially in historical India or Portugal.
examples: The raja issued bandos to his subjects.
Bandos announced new taxes.

senses.definition: A filter that lets certain frequencies pass through.
examples: The bandpass filter improved the audio quality.
Engineers designed a narrow bandpass for the signal.

senses.definition: Plural of bandpass; frequency filters.
examples: The system uses multiple bandpasses.
Bandpasses separate the sound waves.

senses.definition: Variant of banderole, a small flag or banner.
examples: The bandrol fluttered from the mast.
Heraldic bandrols decorated the shield.
